summary: Plan 08 is implementation-ready under the repaired verification-baseline contract. Baseline owns the pre-existing state.py trust evidence, the conservative Wardline 1.3.1 pack bridge and focused external verifier, exact non-inert complete-analysis acceptance, and the operator-only signed-YAML repair receipt. Every verifier and scan invocation now exposes the repository-root config package to Wardline's installed interpreter with PYTHONPATH=$PWD, while the verifier imports the same canonical config.wardline.elspeth_pack module as the real loader. 08A owns only the S3 web endpoint gate.

review_basis.head: a579626e46c97973bc50b5dc9235032ad98199d9
review_basis.baseline: The reviewed program-worktree repair was checked against live Wardline 1.3.1 import behavior. The installed console interpreter requires PYTHONPATH=$PWD to import the repository-root config package, and the focused verifier now explicitly uses importlib.import_module("config.wardline.elspeth_pack") so it exercises the canonical loader route.

warnings.detail: 08A remains blocked by P1 Baseline elspeth-8166b310e7. Baseline dispatch is blocked until the live structured tracker fields are reconciled to this reviewed ownership split.

The final protected diagnosis receipt may name existing signed YAML rows requiring repair. Agents never sign; the coordinator records the exact receipt paths and an operator reviews and repairs only that set under the signing freeze.

The authoritative Wardline gate is pinned to 1.3.1 and requires the Elspeth pack, non-inert recognized coverage, zero unanalyzed source units, zero active ERROR+ defects, and absence of Wardline's formal incomplete-analysis facts. Version/schema drift requires re-review.

Conservative Wardline resolution facts outside the formal incomplete-analysis set remain visible and counted but do not imply a false clean result; the pack deliberately seeds raw input to raw output because the decorator also covers non-raising predicates.

resolved_findings.resolution: Baseline and 08A file ownership is disjoint: state trust evidence, pack/config/verifier, and signed metadata belong to Baseline; S3 web policy code belongs to 08A.

All agent-owned baseline mutations and focused checks precede final signed diagnosis; the signing freeze then permits only exact operator YAML repair, one receipt-bound tracker update, read-only gates, staging, commit, and closure.
The Wardline gate uses agent-summary plus an exact jq assertion for version, gate result, active defects, unanalyzed units, non-inertness, recognized boundaries, and formal incomplete-analysis facts.

The pack contract is checked by a dedicated script executed with Wardline's shebang-derived interpreter and PYTHONPATH=$PWD, outside ordinary pytest collection where Wardline is not a project dependency. The verifier imports config.wardline.elspeth_pack through importlib, matching Wardline's real pack loader.

Final diagnosis is captured in a mode-0600 JSON receipt; safe unique YAML paths are derived mechanically, reconciled into Filigree, staged as an exact array, and compared against the complete expected staged set.

The shared-tip close protocol accepts an implementation ancestor only with a complete fresh handoff on the current exact tip, which makes pre-Baseline Plan 01/02 implementations closable after Baseline without weakening evidence.

Baseline reopen has an executable special case that invalidates all other milestone-step Wardline handoffs, preserves WIP commits/checkpoints, and reopens closed consumers in reverse topological order.

The endpoint policy, central validation/execution backstop, YAML/seed persistence behavior, composer mutation inventory, prompt parity, redaction, and CLI separation from the original approved review remain unchanged.

verification: live Wardline 1.3.1 version, CLI flags, lowercase grammar loader, pack API, FunctionTaint fields, agent-summary fields, and formal incomplete-analysis IDs checked
authoritative jq assertion executed successfully against a current non-inert 56-boundary agent-summary
signed-diagnosis JSON receipt command executed: exit 1 matched requires_action true, safe derivation produced exactly config/cicd/enforce_tier_model/web.yaml, and both receipt files were mode 0600
ordinary CI pytest collection confirmed to omit Wardline as a dependency, motivating the dedicated external-tool verifier outside tests
shared-tip ancestor close and executable Baseline reopen special case reviewed
all Plan 08 verifier and Wardline scan invocations use PYTHONPATH=$PWD and preserve the exact version, trust-pack, non-inert, complete-analysis, and ERROR+ gates
Plan 08 and orchestration run-sheet diff passed git diff --check
independent Wardline contract review returned APPROVED with no security, signing, evidence, or scan-scope weakening

review_history.summary: Initial review found incomplete endpoint-path coverage and hidden signed/Wardline prerequisites.
The endpoint implementation split was approved, with Baseline still described by a stale Wardline budget failure and state evidence assigned to 08A.
Live execution exposed a Baseline/08A ownership cycle and an inert bare Wardline scan; the repaired contract now provides executable ownership, evidence, freeze, close, and reopen semantics.

The repository-root import repair was independently checked against Wardline 1.3.1. All verifier and scan paths now use the canonical config.wardline.elspeth_pack route without weakening fail-closed acceptance.
